commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram reply dispatch to suppress post-final text-only tool or tagged non-terminal warning payloads while preserving terminal final warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +50. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main can be source-reproduced by delivering a final answer followed by a final payload tagged nonTerminalToolErrorWarning; the existing Telegram guard only suppresses post-final tool-kind payloads.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human proof acceptance is the remaining blocker: a maintainer needs to trigger/accept Telegram proof or explicitly override the proof gate.

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress `final` error payloads only when core marks them as `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, and tagged non-terminal final warning suppression

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Resolved the inline review thread after pushing the repair
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `ed4c4afc0f` and squashed into single commit `1a59a77093`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to current head `1a59a77093f9`.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `1a59a77093f9`, matching the PR head.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime is back online after restart and the PR branch remains mergeable with the prior code P1 resolved. Latest Codex review found no major issues; the remaining ClawSweeper blocker is proof acceptance.
- What was not tested by this bot after the squash: I did not run additional local build, test, or restart commands in this turn; Keshav provided the post-squash build/restart confirmation. Previous targeted local verification before the rebase/squash is listed below.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation, not a Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Post-squash maintainer/operator confirmation:
- Built and restarted live OpenClaw Telegram runtime on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC.

## Current review state
- Code P1 is folded into current squashed head `1a59a77093`
- Inline review thread is resolved
- Latest Codex review found no major issues
- Mantis proof request was attempted, but skipped due contributor permission
- Proof has now been updated with maintainer/operator live Telegram build and restart confirmation
- Waiting on ClawSweeper proof acceptance, maintainer-triggered Mantis if still required, or explicit proof override before merge
